summary.brief: Frankel v. Deane. Medical malpractice case involving neurology.

evidence_index.text: Dr. Frankel performed the surgery on January 14, 2016. Ms. Deane signed consent forms and was, according to Dr. Frankel’s medical notes, 'told of possible complications, mainly pain, burning, paresthesia lower lip, chin, tongue.'
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: When Ms. Deane awoke after the surgery, she was in pain and couldn’t speak or feel her tongue.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Ms. Deane had a follow-up appointment with Dr. Frankel several days after the surgery. Dr. Frankel’s notes from the visit reflect that Ms. Deane complained of pain, paresthesia, and tingling on the front third of both sides of her tongue.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Dr. Kramer testified at his deposition that the standard of care for diagnosing nerve injuries was set forth in the 'Nerve Evaluation Protocol 2014' from the California Association of Oral and Maxillofacial Surgeons.
evidence_index.speaker: Dr. Kramer

evidence_index.text: Accordingly, Dr. Kotikian’s opinions as to violations of the ordinary standard of dental care as to all Defendants are not admissible in this matter, and therefore, inter alia, there is not found ultimately to exist a genuine dispute of material facts, such that Plaintiff’s claims do not survive Summary Judgment with respect to all Defendants.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The court determined affirmatively that there was no genuine dispute of material fact that none of the three Defendants committed negligence as alleged for such a failure to so refer.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

initial_state.chief_complaint: Pain and inability to feel tongue after wisdom teeth extraction
initial_state.history_of_present_illness: Patient underwent wisdom teeth extraction and awoke with pain and inability to feel her tongue. Symptoms persisted without improvement.

focused_exam.oral: Patient reports numbness and tingling on the front third of both sides of her tongue

critical_findings: Numbness and inability to feel tongue

reveal.result_summary: Patient complained of pain, paresthesia, and tingling on the front third of both sides of her tongue
reveal.decision_impact: Should prompt further investigation or referral

requestables.name: Consult with Dr. Kim
requestables.description: Consultation with another doctor for persistent symptoms

reveal.result_summary: Patient reported numbness, but notes indicated some improvement
reveal.clinical_significance: Discrepancy in patient report and notes
reveal.decision_impact: Should consider referral to a specialist

decision_points.clinical_context: Patient with persistent numbness and pain post-surgery
decision_points.prompt: Should the patient be referred to a nerve specialist?

options.description: Refer to a nerve specialist immediately
options.clinical_reasoning: Early referral could prevent permanent damage

options.description: Advise patient to wait and observe
options.clinical_reasoning: Assumed symptoms would resolve over time

actual_action_defendant.description: Patient was advised to wait and observe

expected_action_court.description: Referral to a nerve specialist should have been made
expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: Expert testimony indicated early intervention is critical

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Failure to refer led to permanent nerve damage
explanation.what_should_have_happened: Referral to a specialist for early intervention
explanation.legal_standard_applied: Standard of care as established by expert testimony

patient_outcome.description: Permanent loss of sensation in tongue

key_findings: Court found that nerve injury was a known risk of the procedure
Summary judgment was initially granted due to inadmissibility of expert testimony

malpractice_determination.point_of_failure: Failure to use protective measures during surgery
malpractice_determination.counterfactual: Use of protective measures could have prevented nerve injury

tests_performed.result: No feeling in the front two-thirds of both sides of tongue

allegations.allegation: Failure to refer to a nerve specialist
Failure to use protective measures during surgery

court_findings.finding: No genuine dispute of material fact
court_findings.legal_significance: Summary judgment granted in favor of defendants

court_findings.finding: Nerve injury was a known risk of the procedure
court_findings.legal_significance: No breach of standard of care
